evals.prompt: The 3d-troubleshoot skill is loaded. The user says: "trimesh said the mesh is watertight but the slicer shows holes and my print failed." Diagnose and fix.
evals.expected_output: Cause: trimesh silently repairs a copy and reports it watertight (a false positive) — or the mesh came from TRELLIS (open shell) rather than Hunyuan. Fix: verify with a Blender bmesh manifold/boundary audit + backface-cull render; use Hunyuan (watertight by construction) for prints. Does NOT propose a venv/pip change.
expectations: The response names trimesh's silent-repair false positive (and/or using an open-shell TRELLIS mesh for a print) as the cause.
The fix is to verify with a bmesh audit + backface-cull render and to use Hunyuan for prints — not a pip/venv change.

evals.prompt: The 3d-troubleshoot skill is loaded. The user says: "my shape came out as a thin ~57k-triangle sliver." What happened and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: The #1 trap: multiview conditioning was fed to the single-view DiT. Fix: use the dedicated MV model (hunyuan3d-dit-v2-mv) for multiview, or use single-view Hunyuan for a single image. Note that lowering octree can't recover it — the shape is baked at sampling.
expectations: The response identifies multiview conditioning fed to the single-view model as the cause.
The fix is to use the dedicated MV model (or single-view for one image); it notes octree cannot fix an already-collapsed shape.

evals.prompt: The 3d-troubleshoot skill is loaded. The user says: "it OOMs on 16 GB during the decode." What do you change?
evals.expected_output: Causes/levers: don't load shape+texture together (texture is a separate stage — free VRAM between models); keep expandable_segments:True; lower octree_resolution; for multiview use front+back only (4 views OOM). Not a venv change.
expectations: The response gives on-box VRAM levers: separate the texture stage / free VRAM between models, keep expandable_segments, lower octree, and (for MV) fewer views.
The response does not propose editing the ComfyUI venv.

evals.prompt: The 3d-troubleshoot skill is loaded. The user says: "my validation render is empty or the model is lying on its side." What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: A Y-up vs Z-up mismatch, not a mesh failure. STLs from print_prep are Z-up; the glTF importer converts Y-up→Z-up on import — get the camera up-axis right and the render is fine. It misreads as a failure otherwise.
expectations: The response identifies a Y-up vs Z-up axis mismatch (not a broken mesh) as the cause.
The response frames it as a camera/axis fix, noting print_prep STLs are Z-up and glTF import converts Y-up→Z-up.

evals.prompt: The 3d-troubleshoot skill is loaded. The user says: "should I pip install a newer trimesh into the ComfyUI venv to fix the watertight false positives?" Show your response.
evals.expected_output: No — refuses a venv pip install (hard constraint). The watertight false positive is fixed by not trusting trimesh at all (use the bmesh audit + backface-cull render), not by upgrading it. The fix is verification method + engine choice, never a venv edit.
expectations: The response refuses the pip install into the ComfyUI venv (hard constraint).
The response redirects to the real fix: stop trusting trimesh; verify via bmesh audit + backface-cull render.
